description_long: This object consists of two separate sections of rustic wooden fencing, each constructed from horizontal rails supported by vertical posts. The rails are arranged in a staggered, overlapping pattern, giving the fence a rugged, hand-built appearance. The wood is dark brown, with visible grain and a matte finish, suggesting it is untreated or minimally processed. The fence sections are relatively low in height and appear to be designed for demarcation rather than security, suitable for use in parks, gardens, or as decorative barriers in urban environments. There are no visible gates, hinges, or decorative embellishments, and the tops of the posts and rails are flat and unadorned. The construction is simple, with no visible fasteners or hardware, emphasizing a utilitarian and natural aesthetic.
description: A rustic, dark brown wooden fence composed of two low, staggered sections with flat tops and no decorative elements. The design is simple and utilitarian, suitable for marking boundaries in urban parks or gardens.
description_view_0: Two sections of dark brown wooden fence with staggered rails and flat-topped posts are visible from the front, showing the overlapping construction.
description_view_1: The fence is seen from above and slightly to the side, highlighting the thin profile of the rails and the spacing between the two sections.
description_view_2: A top-down view reveals the full length and arrangement of the rails, emphasizing the parallel alignment and the open space between the two fence sections.
description_view_3: A rear angled view shows the back sides of the fence sections, with the same staggered rail pattern and flat-topped posts visible.
